cont_clean: Fraude bancario por spoofing, vishing y smishing ¿Has recibido un SMS, una llamada o un correo que parecía proceder de tu banco y acabaste realizando una transferencia, facilitando un código o entrando en un enlace? Los delincuentes utilizan técnicas cada vez más sofisticadas para hacerse pasar por una entidad bancaria: 📩 Smishing: mensajes SMS con enlaces o avisos falsos. 📞 Vishing: llamadas en las que el estafador suplanta al banco. 🖥️ Spoofing: manipulación del remitente, teléfono o identidad para que la comunicación parezca auténtica. Ante un fraude de este tipo, el banco no queda automáticamente exento de responsabilidad. Es necesario estudiar cómo se autorizó la operación, qué medidas de seguridad se aplicaron y si la entidad actuó con la diligencia exigible. Si te ha ocurrido: ⚠️ Contacta inmediatamente con el banco. ⚠️ Bloquea tarjetas y accesos digitales. ⚠️ Guarda mensajes, llamadas, justificantes y capturas. ⚠️ Presenta denuncia y reclama la devolución por escrito. topic: AI 语音诈骗是否应强制披露或限制？
cont_clean: Il vishing bancario: come riconoscere lo spoofing del numero della banca e cosa fare nelle prime 24 ore Il telefono squilla. Sul display, il numero della vostra banca. Quello vero, salvato in rubrica. Non è la banca. È spoofing: la tecnica che falsifica numero e nome del chiamante. Non è un'intrusione nei sistemi bancari — è un trucco sul protocollo telefonico. Ma funziona, perché il numero visualizzato non è più una prova di nulla. Tre cose che una banca non fa mai per telefono: → non chiede di leggere un codice OTP appena ricevuto → non chiede di installare un'app di assistenza remota → non crea urgenza artificiale ("blocchiamo tra due minuti") Se lo sentite dire, riattaccate. Sempre. Un punto che pochi sanno: la legge (D.Lgs. 11/2010, recepimento PSD2) mette l'onere della prova sulla banca, non su di voi. Deve dimostrare lei che l'operazione era autenticata correttamente e che il codice OTP era collegato in modo univoco a quella specifica operazione (dynamic linking). Un OTP generico o riutilizzabile è già un indizio di carenza del sistema antifrode. Le prime 24 ore, in ordine: Blocco immediato di carte e home banking — telefono, poi PEC Denuncia-querela, citando esplicitamente lo spoofing Richiesta di rimborso alla banca ex art. 12 D.Lgs. 11/2010 Conservare tutto: SMS, orari, log delle chiamate Il tempo gioca due volte contro di voi: la banca ha termini stretti per rispondere, e voi perdete precisione nella ricostruzione dei fatti più aspettate. Vi è mai capitata una chiamata così, anche solo sospetta? Raccontatela nei commenti — in forma anonima, senza nomi — può aiutare qualcun altro a riconoscerla in tempo. #DirittoBancario #ABF #Vishing #TutelaConsumatori

cont_clean: 🚨 ¡ALERTA DE SEGURIDAD! Desconfía si te piden marcar el *21 Los ciberdelincuentes están usando una nueva modalidad de estafa telefónica (Vishing). Se hacen pasar por ejecutivos bancarios o soporte técnico y, bajo una falsa urgencia (como un supuesto fraude en tu cuenta), te solicitan marcar el código *21 seguido de un número de teléfono. ¿Qué pasa si lo haces? Al marcar ese código, activas el desvío de llamadas hacia el teléfono del atacante. Con esto, ellos pueden: • Interceptar tus llamadas telefónicas. • Recibir tus códigos de verificación por voz (2FA). • Validar transferencias bancarias y tomar el control de tus cuentas. 🛡️ ¿Cómo protegerte? 1. Ninguna institución legítima te pedirá marcar códigos en tu celular para solucionar un problema. 2. Si te genera dudas, cuelga de inmediato y llama tú mismo al número oficial de tu banco. 3. Si crees que caíste en el engaño, marca ##21# en tu celular para desactivar de inmediato cualquier desvío de llamadas activo. ¡Comparte este ciberconsejo con tu familia y amigos, especialmente con los adultos mayores! cont_clean: This new Commentary by Fatih Aktoz, Nikolaos P Polyzos, and Christophe Blockeel explains what lies "inside the box" of regression analysis in IVF research. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.rbmo.2026.105800 In IVF studies, where outcomes are influenced by many biological and clinical factors, regression models offer a structured way to address complexity. However, an adjusted result is not automatically more reliable than a crude comparison. The meaning of a regression estimate depends on the outcome chosen, the variables included, the assumptions made, and the structure of the data. Without understanding these elements, even a statistically impressive model may mislead. In the same way that a single p value cannot capture the full meaning of a study, a single regression model cannot define clinical reality. A regression model is a structured summary of data built on specific assumptions. For this reason, it should not be seen as the final answer, but as one more step toward understanding. topic: IVF 等辅助生殖治疗是否应获得更多公共支持？
cont_clean: De 3 typiske fertilitetsbehandlingsformer er IVF, ICSI og IUI. IVF står for "In Vitro Fertilisering", også kendt under daglig tale som "reagensglasbefrugtning". IVF dækker over to typer: 1) den konventionelle, klassiske IVF, hvor sædceller og ægceller placeres i en petriskål. 2) ICSI, forkortet fra "Intra Cytoplasmatisk Sædcelle Injektion", eller også kendt som mikroinsemination, indebærer at én sædcelle sprøjtes med en tynd nål direkte ind i ægcellen. Således er denne type IVF altså endnu mere assisteret end den konventionelle. IUI (Intrauterine Insemination) er klassisk insemination, og foregår dermed ikke "in vitro" men derimod "in vivo". IVF, ICSI og IUI henviser til selve måden hvorpå befrugtningen sker. Ved IVF/ICSI er det (befrugtningen) en mindre del af et større billede. Først bliver kvindens æggestokke og æg stimuleret med hormoner, så der modnes flere æg end naturligt. Derefter følger en ægudtagning, hvor forhåbningen er at få flere æg ud på én gang, så man har flere forsøg til at få befrugtningen til at lykkes. Herefter følger der en protokol for forberedelsen af både æg og sædceller, som involverer rensning samt udvælgelse af celler med bedst kvalitet. Befrugtningen sker enten ved konventionel IVF eller ICSI, hvorefter udviklingen af det befrugtede æg - nu kaldet embryo - følges tæt. Når (eller hvis) embryonet har udviklet sig til blastocyst-stadiet, på ca. 5. dagen, bliver embryoet 'transfereret' ind i kvinden. Dette kaldes også ægoplægning eller embryo transfer, hvor man håber på implantation - altså at ægget sætter sig fast i livmoderen. Dette bliver typisk først bekræftet ved en efterfølgende graviditetstest. #ivftreatment #invitrofertilization #icsi #assistedreproductivetechnology #fertilitytreatment

cont_clean: Booked a Phoenix or Scottsdale resort lately and the 'final' price jumped $80/night above the listing? That's now illegal. And you can get the money back. 💳 FTC's Junk Fees rule (effective May 12, 2025) + AZ Consumer Fraud Act (A.R.S. § 44-1521) require: the ADVERTISED price must include every MANDATORY fee. If you can't book without paying the fee, it has to be in the up-front price — not after your card is entered. Applies to: • Hotels + resorts (no surprise 'resort fees') • Short-term rentals (cleaning, 'destination' fees) • Live-event tickets (Ticketmaster, SeatGeek, StubHub) • Vacation rentals What's covered: resort, cleaning, service, processing, facility fees ✅ What's NOT: truly optional add-ons like parking, early check-in, upgrades. How to get a refund: 1) SCREENSHOT immediately — listed price, cart, final receipt 2) Email the vendor: 'Your advertised rate was $X. My mandatory total was $Y. Under the FTC Junk Fees rule and A.R.S. § 44-1521, please refund the difference.' Most chains refund at this step. 3) If denied: file at reportfraud.ftc.gov AND azag.gov/consumer. Cite the rule by name, attach screenshots. 4) Credit card chargeback as backstop ('service not as described'). AZ note: AG Mayes has publicly committed to enforcing this against AZ properties AND national vendors for AZ-resident bookings. You don't need to prove the company is HQ'd in AZ. Worst surprise fee you got hit with in AZ this year? cont_clean: 🏥 **No toda dolencia necesita emergencia** Cuando un hijo se siente mal, es normal asustarse y pensar rápido: "¿lo llevo a emergencia?". Pero en Estados Unidos, ir al ER por algo que no es urgente puede terminar en una factura muy alta. A veces hay otras opciones: **urgent care, clínica comunitaria, pediatra, telehealth o la línea de enfermería del seguro**. 😟 La sala de emergencia es para situaciones serias: dificultad para respirar, dolor fuerte en el pecho, síntomas de derrame, desmayo, sangrado fuerte, convulsiones, dolor intenso, golpes graves o señales de peligro real. Si la vida está en riesgo, no se duda: **llama al 911 o ve a emergencia**. Pero para síntomas más leves, como fiebre moderada, dolor de garganta, infección de oído, alergias, torceduras simples o malestar que no parece grave, un **urgent care** puede ser una alternativa más rápida y menos costosa. También existen clínicas comunitarias que atienden según ingresos o con tarifas reducidas. 📌 **Antes de decidir, revisa:** ✅ qué tan graves son los síntomas ✅ si hay dificultad para respirar o dolor fuerte ✅ si tu seguro tiene nurse line 24/7 ✅ urgent care cercanos y horarios ✅ clínicas comunitarias en tu área ✅ cuánto puede ser el copay o deductible Según reportes de costos de salud, una visita a urgent care suele ser mucho más barata que una visita a emergency room, especialmente para problemas no graves. Y aunque el hospital debe atender emergencias, eso no significa que la factura desaparezca. 💡 Consejo práctico: guarda en tu celular una lista con el pediatra, urgent care cercanos, clínica comunitaria, farmacia 24 horas y número del seguro. En un momento de preocupación, tener esa información lista puede ayudarte a decidir mejor. Cuidar la salud también es cuidar el bolsillo. 🙌 💬 **¿Alguna vez fuiste a emergencia y después pensaste que tal vez había otra opción?** #DeEsteLado #SaludEnUSA #FacturasMedicas #UrgentCare #Emergencia #FamiliaHispana #HispanosEnUSA #LatinosEnUSA #EconomiaFamiliar #ConsejosParaLatinos #VidaEnEstadosUnidos #SaludFamiliar #MedicalBills #ComunidadHispana

cont_clean: For years, CWFIA has challenged the narrative that SNAP fraud and improper payments were only around 1%. The latest USDA data shows why that claim never passed the smell test. In FY2025, states and territories made an estimated $10.1 billion in improper SNAP payments, with a national payment error rate of 10.62%, far above the 6% federal threshold. With total SNAP spending reaching roughly $101.7 billion, these payment errors represent about 10% of the program's entire budget. While improper payments are not the same as proven fraud, these numbers expose how misleading it has been to dismiss concerns about waste, abuse, and weak program integrity by pointing to an alleged 1% fraud rate. The reality is that billions of taxpayer dollars are being distributed inaccurately, and taxpayers deserve accountability and transparency. CWFIA has long warned that the scope of misuse in SNAP is substantially larger than official talking points suggest, and this USDA report is further evidence that the problem is far more serious than many policymakers have been willing to acknowledge. The proposal sought to prevent SNAP recipients from using benefits to purchase items such as soda, candy, and other products critics describe as "junk food." U.S. District Judge Amy Berman Jackson ruled that the USDA does not have the authority to redefine what qualifies as "food" under the SNAP program without action from Congress. Supporters of the restrictions argue that taxpayer-funded assistance programs should encourage healthier choices and not subsidize products linked to obesity, diabetes, and other health problems. Critics of the proposal counter that SNAP recipients should have the same freedom to make food choices as everyone else and that decisions about eligible purchases should be made by Congress, not federal agencies acting on their own. The ruling leaves a larger question unanswered: Should government assistance programs place limits on what recipients can buy, or should individuals decide for themselves how to spend their benefits? Verbote greifen oft zu kurz, sie verlagern Nutzung ins Verborgene und erschweren den Dialog. Was es stattdessen braucht: • Aufklärung und Sensibilisierung • aktive Begleitung • Räume für Austausch 👉 Wie seht ihr das — mehr Kontrolle oder mehr Begleitung? cont_clean: About to set up your child's first email address? Please read this before you do. I know it feels like a small thing. A little Gmail account so they can log into their school apps, maybe a game or two. But the moment your child's email address goes into a sign-up form — even a "safe" kids' website — it enters a system that is designed to share it. Not maliciously. Just routinely. That's just how it works. And once their address is out there, the spam starts. Then the marketing. Then the stuff you really don't want landing in your 10-year-old's inbox. Here's what I'd do differently if I were starting over. Set your child up with an alias system from day one. Every app they join gets its own unique email address. Every game. Every school platform. Every single one. All of them forward to one inbox you can monitor together. If anything weird starts coming through, you see exactly which platform sent it. You delete that address instantly. It's gone. They're protected. It's the digital equivalent of teaching your child not to give their real name to strangers — but for email. cont_clean: As of 3 April 2026, the EU became the only jurisdiction in the world where tech companies no longer have a clear legal basis to scan their platforms for child sexual abuse material. This isn't because the threat disappeared, far from it. It's because lawmakers failed to act in time! What makes this particularly striking is that child protection organisations and technology companies, two groups that rarely agree on anything, stood together and asked the European Parliament to extend the temporary framework that kept detection tools running. 246 civil society organisations across every EU member state signed a joint condemnation and industry followed, but the Parliament voted against the extension anyway. To be clear about what this law actually was, it wasn't mass surveillance, nor a backdoor into private messages. Rather, it allowed companies to voluntarily use privacy-preserving tools like digital fingerprint matching, to detect known abuse images and flag grooming attempts. It was targeted, voluntary and effective. Now those tools have no legal basis to operate in the EU. A permanent Child Sexual Abuse Regulation has been in negotiation since 2022. It is still deadlocked. Until it passes, there is a gap and predators are aware of it long before most parents are. This moment is a reminder of something important: we cannot fully outsource our children's safety to governments. Policy matters. Legislation matters, but when politics stalls, children are the ones left exposed. The most powerful protection a child has online is a parent or guardian who understands the risks, knows the platforms their children use, and creates a space where children feel safe to speak up. Talk to them. Not once, often. Ask questions, stay curious. Build the kind of trust that means they come to you when something feels wrong. Legislation will eventually catch up, but your child needs you now. #OnlineChildSafety #ChildProtection #EUSafety #DigitalParenting #fypage

cont_clean: ⚖️ Madhya Pradesh High Court | Judgment dated 3 July 2026 A significant ruling by Justice Sandeep N. Bhatt has reaffirmed that a married woman does not need her husband's consent to terminate a pregnancy. In this case, a woman seeking termination of her 13-week and 1-day pregnancy approached the High Court as she feared her estranged husband might later challenge the procedure. Although notice was issued, the husband did not appear, and the State raised no objection, relying on Supreme Court precedents recognising women's reproductive autonomy. The Court held that since the pregnancy was within the 20-week limit under the Medical Termination of Pregnancy Act, 1971, a registered medical practitioner could legally perform the termination even without a court order. Reaffirming Article 21, the Court held that reproductive autonomy, bodily integrity, dignity, privacy, and informed consent belong to the woman alone. The petition was allowed, and the Court directed that the procedure be carried out in accordance with the Medical Termination of Pregnancy Act, 1971 and applicable medical guidelines. cont_clean: Pro-Reproductive Care because healthcare decisions belong in medicine, not ideology. Women deserve access to timely, evidence-based care, whether miscarriage management in which the pregnancy has already ended naturally, (a spontaneous abortion) but medication helps the uterus safely expel the remaining pregnancy tissue; or medical abortion, in which an ongoing pregnancy is intentionally terminated because continuing the pregnancy poses a serious medical risk to the mother. In some circumstances, a pregnancy must be aborted because the fetus has a condition incompatible with life. Many people believe that banning elective abortion does not affect miscarriage care and medically necessary abortions, and few could imagine how these laws could result in the denial of postpartum care. However, the road to hell is paved with good intentions. Unfortunately, the ban on elective abortion prohibits or delays access to medications, procedures and clinical decisions necessary during pregnancy loss, pregnancy complications, and even postpartum care because doctors fear legal consequences. Restrictions on abortion can have ripple effects beyond elective abortions because the same medication's and procedures are used in multiple areas of reproductive medicine. Essential treatment may be delayed until a mother's condition worsens or she dies. Too many of us have been denied miscarriage care and even postpartum care due to legal uncertainty surrounding abortion bans in states like Idaho, Arkansas, Texas and Georgia. In June 2026, Congresswoman Ayanna Pressley led a moment of silence on the house floor reading the names of women who have died after being denied or delayed abortion related emergency care. Among the women remembered are Amber Nicole Thurman, Candi Miller, Ciji Graham, Josseli Barnica, Yeniifer Alvarez-Estrada Glick, Nevaeh Crain, Tierra Walker, Taysha Wilkinson-Sobieski, Porsha Ngumezi and Adriana Smith. Say their names. #abortion #medicalabortion #prolife #prochoiceisprolife #reproductiverights

The judge made something very clear: her consent alone is sufficient under the law - she does not require her estranged husband's consent or the court's permission to undergo the procedure. This matters because of what it tells us about how women still approach their own bodies in India. She filed a petition - not because the law required it - but because she was afraid her husband would challenge the termination later. She came to court as a precaution against a man who had already stopped treating her as an equal. The court saw that. And it used the opportunity to say something louder than a dismissal order. A woman's reproductive choice is not a negotiation. It is not subject to a husband's approval. It is not something a court needs to authorize within the limits already set by law. It is a fundamental right - protected under Article 21 of the Constitution of India - that belongs entirely to her. She didn't need his permission. She never did. The law said so in 1971. The court just made sure she heard it in 2026. #WomensRights #ReproductiveRights #MadhyaPradeshHighCourt #IndianLaw #Article21

cont_clean: Limerence is the epitome of an attachment. It is when the limerent individual (LI) becomes involuntarily obsessed and attached to another person (LO), resulting in all other relationships, responsibilities, and priorities to be put on the back burner. The attachment that limerence creates can quickly take control of the LI's life, leaving them feeling isolated, confused, and empty. Here are some tips of how NOT to stay attached… • Try not being available as much for your LO. When you start creating NEW habits of not being available, these new habits replace the old of always being there. • Schedule your day around YOU, not them. There may be a pull to revolve everything around them but try to make a goal of revolving at least one aspect of your day around you and what you have to do. Gradually increase this goal. • Focus on YOURSELF to replace being needy. The more we are needy, the more we are taken for granted. When you focus on YOURSELF and what YOU NEED, you retrain your brain to prioritize yourself. • Stop fearing losing them. If they have the same value for you in their life, you will never lose them. If they don't, they weren't worth it in the first place. • Distract yourself when you find yourself thinking of them constantly. Physically move your body, start a puzzle, call a friend. Observe your thoughts without latching on to them. Overcoming limerence entails creating NEW habits to replace your limerent ones. Replacing the limerent habits involves DETACHING from the need to stay attached to your limerent object (LO). Remind yourself that you weren't always attached or limerent towards your LO and you were OKAY… it will be the SAME WAY when you detach from them. What resonates with you? Rapid IV push fentanyl can lock the chest wall — ventilation becomes difficult or impossible, the patient can't be bagged effectively. It happens fast. It can be mistaken for something else if you don't know to look for it. Watch for: slowing RR, sudden rigidity, difficult ventilation, decreased responsiveness, EtCO2 changes. Do not rely on SpO2 alone — it lags. Sedation happens before respiratory arrest. Your eyes catch it before the monitor does. If it happens: stop immediately. Call for help. Support the airway. cont_clean: FDA Refuses to Set Tolerance Levels for Toxic PFAS "Forever Chemicals" in Food: The FDA has rejected a legal petition demanding it set limits on toxic PFAS in food, marking another setback for public health advocates' push to limit exposures to the dangerous compounds. The agency is refusing to set limits despite a growing body of science and the EPA finding that food is the biggest source of PFAS exposure. Testing has found the levels of PFAS in single servings of some contaminated foods to be equivalent to drinking many glasses of contaminated water. Recent FDA testing found 70% of seafood samples contain the chemicals, while independent milk testing found it in 12% of 50 samples, including extremely high levels in Whole Foods and Kirkland Signature brands. Meat and crops produced on farms that use sewage sludge (which are most conventional farms) have been found by independent tests to contain high levels of PFAS, and some state agencies have ordered contaminated food and milk to be taken off the market. Independent testing has found high levels of PFAS in blueberries, kale and other water-rich produce because the chemicals are attracted to water. cont_clean: The Hidden Cost of PBMs 💰 Who Is Really Driving Up Healthcare Costs? Most patients assume their insurance company determines what they pay for prescriptions. In reality, a large portion of prescription drug pricing is controlled by Pharmacy Benefit Managers (PBMs)—middlemen that operate between drug manufacturers, insurers, pharmacies, and patients. Here's the problem: ⚠️ Independent pharmacies are often reimbursed below their acquisition cost for medications. ⚠️ Patients are frequently steered toward PBM-owned mail-order and specialty pharmacies. ⚠️ Prescription drug costs continue to rise while PBM profits reach record levels. ⚠️ Taxpayer-funded programs, including Medicare and Medicaid, may pay inflated costs due to opaque rebate and pricing practices. ⚠️ Community pharmacies are closing at an alarming rate, reducing patient access to healthcare—especially in rural communities. Every time a local pharmacy closes: ❌ Patients lose access to personalized care. ❌ Healthcare deserts expand. ❌ Competition decreases. ❌ Healthcare costs often increase. The question isn't whether PBMs play a role in healthcare. The question is: Why are the companies controlling prescription drug benefits often the same companies profiting from the prescription supply chain? Patients deserve transparency. Unfortunately, that's often not the case. 📊 Recent surveys from the National Community Pharmacists Association (NCPA) paint a troubling picture: 🔹 In 2024, more than half of independent pharmacies reported being reimbursed below their cost to purchase a medication on at least 30% of prescriptions filled. 🔹 In a 2025 Medicare Part D-focused survey, 40.8% of independent pharmacists reported being paid below acquisition cost on more than 40% of prescriptions, while 29.2% said they lost money on at least half of all prescriptions dispensed. 🔹 For GLP-1 medications used for diabetes and weight management, 95% of pharmacists who dispense these medications reported losing money due to reimbursement rates that fail to cover the pharmacy's cost. cont_clean: Groton hired an outside law firm to investigate its police. The real question isn't who the investigators are. It's who controls the investigation. The Town of Groton has retained Daigle Law Group to investigate an internal affairs complaint filed by First Amendment auditor SeanPaul Reyes following his July 2025 arrest outside the home of retired Connecticut State Police Sgt. Bryan Fahey. The incident drew national attention after video appeared to show Fahey, while armed, spitting in Reyes's face during the confrontation. Reyes originally faced multiple criminal charges. Most have since been dismissed, leaving a single charge of third-degree criminal trespass pending. Fahey was later charged with disorderly conduct. Both cases remain before the court. Now comes the internal investigation. An outside law firm can absolutely provide an objective review. It can also become another layer between government officials and public accountability. That's why the public shouldn't simply assume "outside counsel" means independence. Who determines the scope of the investigation? Who decides what evidence investigators receive? Can investigators interview anyone they believe is relevant, or only those the town identifies? Does Groton Police get an opportunity to dispute or edit factual findings before the report is released? Will taxpayers receive the full report, or only a heavily redacted version? Those answers matter far more than the firm's name. If taxpayers are paying for this investigation, they deserve complete transparency, including the engagement letter, investigative scope, billing records, body camera footage, dispatch audio, evidence preservation instructions, internal communications, and the final report. Accountability isn't created by outsourcing an investigation. It's created by allowing the evidence-not the agency being investigated-to determine the outcome. #GrotonCT #PoliceAccountability #FirstAmendment #PublicRecords #CourtWatch

cont_clean: Ever had a UPI payment die at a crowded shop, or a call drop in traffic? India's 5G is blazing fast when the network's quiet and shaky when it's packed. Airtel's new Priority Postpaid is the first fix: "network slicing," a reserved lane on the same network that stays reliable even in a crush. Think of a stadium — 60,000 fans posting reels, a broadcaster's 4K feed, and police who can't afford a dropped signal, all sharing one tower with different guarantees. China's already a step ahead, slicing per-app on a single phone. It moved first because it has 4x the towers, a walled-off home-grown internet, and state-backed telcos — while India's cheap-data war left our operators with little to reinvest. The catch is net neutrality. Speeding up all video calls is fair; prioritising the one app that paid the telco is not. That's the line India has to walk — and if it gets it right, UPI, telemedicine, Kavach and small factories all win. cont_clean: The open internet is starting to feel less human. Axios reported from Axios House in Cannes that media and advertising leaders are warning about bots, AI scraping, and AI-mediated consumption putting pressure on the economics of the open web. The simple version: bots and AI systems are changing how online attention moves. For years, the web was built around human clicks. Someone searches. Someone scrolls. Someone lands on a site. A publisher, creator, or business earns attention. AI changes that loop. If an assistant summarizes the answer, fewer people may click through. If bots scrape content, the creator of the work may not get the same traffic back. If more activity becomes machine-to-machine, the old signals get noisy. This matters beyond media companies. Every business that depends on being found online should be watching this. My read: the next advantage is not just posting more. It is becoming easier to verify, cite, understand, and follow across channels. Clear website. Clear offer. Clear source of truth. Owned audience. Useful content that can survive outside one algorithm. cont_clean: Free school meals have existed in some form in the UK for over a century, but eligibility has always been restricted to the most financially stretched households. Under the previous system, children in Universal Credit households only qualified if their household take home pay after tax and National Insurance was £7,400 a year or less. That threshold was widely criticised as too low, catching only the very poorest families while leaving out large numbers of households who were still genuinely struggling. From September 2026, that threshold is gone entirely for Universal Credit households. Every child whose family receives Universal Credit will now qualify for free school meals, regardless of how much the household earns on top of their benefit entitlement. The change was announced as part of the 2025 Budget and is expected to extend free school meals to around 700,000 additional children who were previously excluded purely because their household income sat above the £7,400 line. For families affected, the saving is real and immediate. Free school meals are worth around £500 per child per year based on current meal costs. For a family with two children both previously ineligible, that's up to £1,000 a year back in the household budget. The policy sits within a broader set of measures the government has described as part of its mission to reduce child poverty, alongside the scrapping of the two child benefit cap from April 2026 and the above inflation rise in Universal Credit standard allowances. Critics of the extension have questioned the cost at a time when the public finances are under significant pressure, and whether extending eligibility to working families on Universal Credit, some of whom earn significant incomes on top of their benefit entitlement, represents the most targeted use of the available funding. Supporters point out that food poverty and hunger in schools has been a documented problem affecting children's ability to learn and concentrate, and that the previous £7,400 threshold created an arbitrary cliff edge #UKPolitics #FreeSchoolMeals #ChildPoverty #PersonalFinance #KnowTheRules

cont_clean: A few weeks ago, something happened in the mental health world that made a lot of providers stop and pay attention. You may have seen it mentioned. If you didn't, let me loop you in, because it could impact you too. But if you have ever wondered why it takes so long for a therapist to get credentialed with an insurance company, or why some providers seem to disappear from insurance directories, CAQH is part of that story. CAQH has been the central database behind provider credentialing in the United States for over 25 years. It holds records on more than 4.8 million providers. It connects eligibility information for more than 75% of covered lives in this country. When an insurance company decides whether to credential a provider, whether to list them in a directory, whether a patient can find them at all, CAQH is the infrastructure underneath that process. Until recently, CAQH operated as a nonprofit. In January 2026, that changed. CAQH announced it is now owned by a consortium of twelve shareholder companies representing the nation's largest health plans, including UnitedHealth Group, Cigna, Aetna, Elevance Health, Humana, and a coalition of Blue Cross Blue Shield plans. A UnitedHealth Group executive now chairs the board. Last month, CAQH rebranded as DataSpring. We are not here to tell you what to think about this. We are here to make sure you know it happened. This database is the front door to your insurance network. Delays and blocks inside it can affect whether a therapist is able to take your insurance. And it's now owned by the largest insurance companies in the country. What we can say from our own experience is that the credentialing process is already one of the biggest structural barriers to expanding access to mental health care in communities like ours. Independent practices like Philos Therapeutic navigate this system every time we try to accept a new insurance and reach more people. Who owns that system matters, especially when you're struggling to find care. Link in bio 🔗 #PhilosTherapeutic #DataSpring #CAQH #MentalHealthAccess #CentralValley TheGapNobodyTalksAbout

The ADA wasn't the end of the fight, it was a turning point. More than 30 years later, millions of people with disabilities and chronic illnesses still face barriers to healthcare, employment, accommodations, and simply being believed. For many living with endometriosis and other complex chronic conditions, the challenge isn't just the disease. cont_clean: EV Tax Credit ⚡ Up to $7,500 back—if you qualify. How the Credit Works: The federal EV tax credit offers up to $7,500 for qualifying new electric vehicles. The credit reduces your tax bill dollar-for-dollar. But not every EV or buyer qualifies. Vehicle Requirements: The vehicle must be assembled in North America and meet battery and mineral sourcing rules. Each requirement is worth $3,750. Failing one cuts the credit in half. Income Limits Apply: The credit phases out at higher incomes. Single filers above $150K, head of household above $225K, and joint filers above $300K may lose eligibility entirely. Price Caps Matter: New EVs must be priced under $55,000 (cars) or $80,000 (SUVs, trucks, vans) to qualify. Exceeding the cap disqualifies the vehicle—even if you qualify otherwise. Used EV Credit Available: Used EVs may qualify for up to $4,000. Lower price caps and income limits apply. This opens EV credits to more buyers. Contact Uncle Kam for EV tax credit guidance. cont_clean: Proper storage reduces the risk of accidental discharge, prevents unauthorized access, and helps protect children and other household members. Start by always storing firearms unloaded when not in use, and keep ammunition locked in a separate, secure location. Use a gun safe, lockbox, or trigger lock that meets your needs—biometric, keypad, or mechanical locks all offer effective protection when used correctly. Poor waste disposal can: Create breeding grounds for mosquitoes that spread malaria. Jeff Rose, PhD, associate professor in the Department of Parks, Recreation, and Tourism, looks at how extreme heat affects our unhoused neighbors in Salt Lake City. His research shows that heat exposure is not just about the weather. It is also about access to shade, water, transit, public spaces, businesses and places where people can safely cool down. cont_clean: You can spend $18 on organic kale… …and still breathe toxic air all day. You can avoid seed oils. Buy "clean" supplements. Drink greens powders. Skip food dyes. Wear a continuous glucose monitor even though you don't have diabetes. Then go for a 10-mile run when the Air Quality Index (AQI) is hazardous. Sometimes I think we've lost perspective. As physicians, we're trained to prioritize risk—not what's trending, but what actually moves the needle for health. You breathe roughly 20,000 times every day. The air you breathe may be one of the most important environmental exposures you'll ever have. When wildfire smoke fills the air, you're inhaling PM2.5—microscopic particles about 30 times smaller than the width of a human hair. They're small enough to reach the deepest parts of your lungs, enter your bloodstream, and trigger inflammation throughout your body. This isn't just about coughing. Air pollution has been linked to: ❤️ Heart attacks 🧠 Stroke 🫁 Asthma attacks 🫁 COPD flare-ups 👶 Pregnancy complications ⚰️ Premature death Yet many people will spend hours debating food dyes or detoxes while never checking the AQI before taking their kids to soccer practice. That's backwards. Health isn't determined by one "superfood" or one "toxic ingredient." It's also shaped by the environment around us: 🌎 The air we breathe 💧 The water we drink 🌡️ Extreme heat 🏞️ Green spaces 🚶 Walkable communities 🍎 Access to healthy food These are called the social and environmental determinants of health, and they have a profound impact on how long—and how well—we live. The irony? Some people dismiss environmental health as "political" while spending hundreds of dollars every month trying to optimize their health. Your lungs don't care about politics. Your heart doesn't care about ideology. Your body responds to the environment it's given. Whether we're talking about cleaner air, cleaner water, reducing pollution, or designing healthier communities, these aren't just environmental issues. They're public health issues. Healthier environments create healthier people. That's not politics. That's medicine.

গত তিন দিন ধরে কুড়িগ্রামের রৌমারী সীমান্তের 'নো ম্যানস ল্যান্ড' বা শূন্যরেখায় খোলা আকাশের নিচে চরম অনিশ্চয়তার মধ্যে দিন কাটাচ্ছেন। ঘটনার পটভূমি ও বর্তমান পরিস্থিতি: কুড়িগ্রামের রৌমারী উপজেলার শৌলমারী ইউনিয়নের গয়টাপাড়া সীমান্তের আন্তর্জাতিক ১০৬০ নম্বর মেইন পিলারের কাছে এক অমানবিক দৃশ্য তৈরি হয়েছে। গত রবিবার (১৪ জুন) ভোরে ভারতের ঝালুরচর বিএসএফ ক্যাম্পের সদস্যরা দুই শিশু, এক নারী ও তিন পুরুষসহ মোট ছয়জন বাংলাদেশিকে পুশ-ইন (পুশ ব্যাক) করার চেষ্টা চালায়। স্থানীয় বাসিন্দা ও বিজিবির তাৎক্ষণিক সতর্ক অবস্থানের কারণে অনুপ্রবেশ ঠেকানো সম্ভব হলেও, এই ছয় ব্যক্তি এখন দুই দেশের সীমান্তরক্ষী বাহিনীর কঠোর নজরদারির মধ্যে শূন্যরেখায় অবরুদ্ধ হয়ে পড়েছেন। চরম খাদ্য ও মানবিক সংকট: বিগত তিন দিন ধরে খোলা আকাশের নিচে থাকা এই পরিবারটির অবস্থা অত্যন্ত আশঙ্কাজনক। শিশুদের আর্তনাদ: সুমি আক্তারের ৫ বছর বয়সী বড় মেয়ে ফাতেমা এবং কোলে থাকা ছোট শিশু ফাহিমা খাদ্যাভাব ও অপুষ্টিতে ভুগছে। সুমি আক্তার জানিয়েছেন, না খেয়ে থাকার কারণে বুকের দুধ শুকিয়ে যাওয়ায় তিনি তার ছোট সন্তানটিকে শান্ত করতে পারছেন না। মানবাধিকার লঙ্ঘন: চারদিকে দুই দেশের বাহিনী ও উৎসুক জনতার উপস্থিতির কারণে নারীদের জন্য প্রকৃতির ডাকে সাড়া দেওয়ার মতো মৌলিক প্রয়োজন মেটানোও অসম্ভব হয়ে পড়েছে। স্থানীয়দের দেওয়া সামান্য চিঁড়ে-মুড়ি আর পানি খেয়ে কোনোমতে বেঁচে আছেন তারা। ভুক্তভোগীদের পরিচয় ও স্বীকারোক্তি: আটকে পড়া ব্যক্তিরা হলেন—ময়মনসিংহের ভালুকা উপজেলার বিরুনিয়া এলাকার কংশেরকুল গ্রামের বিল্লাল হোসেন (৩৫), তাঁর স্ত্রী সুমি আক্তার (২৫), তাঁদের দুই সন্তান ফাতেমা ও ফাহিমা, এবং একই গ্রামের সজীব মিয়া (২৬) ও হিমেল মিয়া। গ্রাম পুলিশ সদস্য মোশারফ হোসেন গণমাধ্যমকে নিশ্চিত করেছেন যে, এরা সবাই বাংলাদেশি নাগরিক এবং কাজের খোঁজে কিছুদিন আগে সীমান্ত পার হয়েছিলেন। ভুক্তভোগী বিল্লাল হোসেন আক্ষেপ করে বলেন, "দালালের খপ্পরে পড়ে কাজের আশায় ভারতে গিয়েছিলাম। সেখানে আটক হওয়ার পর বিএসএফ আমাদের কাঁটাতার পার করে দেয়। আমরা এ দেশেরই নাগরিক।" বিজিবি ও প্রশাসনের অবস্থান: On the morning of July 7, ICE agents in an unmarked car chased & stopped the 52-year-old father of three before shooting him in the abdomen. Salgado Araujo died of his wounds in the hospital. While federal authorities claimed that Salgado Araujo attempted to run over agents, three passengers in his car, who were detained by ICE, said that was false. The Dept. of Homeland Security had also accused Renee Good, fatally shot by an ICE agent in Minneapolis in January, of attempting to run over officers, though video evidence did not substantiate that claim. Less than a week after Salgado Araujo's killing, ICE shot & killed 26-year-old father Joan Sebastian Guerrero in Biddeford, ME, on July 13. At the time of this post, ICE & Border Patrol agents have killed 11 people since Trump's second term began. After initially saying the city had no jurisdiction in the killing of Salgado Araujo, Mayor Whitmire said Friday that he was directing Houston Police Chief Diaz to be "proactive" in investigating Salgado Araujo's shooting. Other authorities—Harris County District Attorney Sean Teare & Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum—announced their intent to investigate the incident in the absence of federal oversight or cooperation. Several orgs are demanding that the city of Houston take a more bold approach toward attaining answers for the Salgado family. Over the course of a week, thousands of residents attended protests & vigils at the site of killing in Houston's East End. cont_clean: Most people look at the total on their electric bill and think about kilowatt hours. But the real story is in the line items most people scroll right past. Utilities recover costs in ways that aren't immediately obvious when you're reading your bill. Fuel adjustment charges. When natural gas prices spike, utilities pass that cost through an automatic fuel charge — separate from your base rate. It adjusts monthly and can move your bill significantly without any formal rate increase. Storm recovery surcharges. In Florida, customers of Duke Energy and Tampa Electric are currently paying added charges to recover hurricane repair costs. This isn't a traditional rate increase — it's a separate line item that can last years. Infrastructure riders. Some utilities get permission to recover capital spending on new power lines and substations through a separate charge rather than waiting for a full rate case. These stack on top of your base rate. Return on equity adjustments. Investor-owned utilities are allowed to earn a set return for shareholders. When they argue that return isn't high enough, they file for increases. Florida Power & Light recently filed for nearly $9 billion citing inadequate shareholder returns. Every one of these costs flows through your bill. And they compound over time. Solar doesn't eliminate your utility connection, but it reduces what you owe them to as little as a fixed monthly grid fee — typically $10–$30 per month in most markets — regardless of how much you generate. Want to understand what your bill would look like after solar? cont_clean: Over the last few months, #Italy has been criticized—for good reasons—for seeking to weaken the #ETS ambitions. And, just to make things very clear, while I completely agree that the ETS (and its future iterations) is the only way forward for our energy transition and climate action objectives, my case-study trip to the amazing #Palermo last week painted a much more complex picture, one shaped by challenges that are very different from those faced in Northern Italy (or most of Western Europe). Palermo is a perfect example of where ETS funds ought to be directed and absorbed, given its aging vehicle fleet; its energy-inefficient housing stock (some would say there is no winter to protect people from, but increasingly frequent and intense heatwaves are creating even greater challenges for building insulation and cooling); and its population's exposure to both economic exclusion and climate stress. Yet little to no progress at scale can be seen on the ground, despite some successful projects (e.g, CER Palermo Est and other energy communities) and despite the city's ambition to become a growing hub for urban sustainability. The issue of #carbonpricing for the general population is not merely a matter of affordability or ideology. It is also a matter of trust in political leadership—whether at the EU, national, or local level—and confidence that resources will be well spent. While many people do not believe (/don't know) that ETS funds are being returned to their economies through climate-positive measures, others simply lack trust in the authorities' capacity to convert these funds into tangible benefits. In other words, while consumers are expected to pay immediately for every kWh associated with carbon emissions they use, the benefits remain diffuse and depend on political leaders' willingness and capacity to implement clean-energy solutions and/or provide end users with the instruments they need to make informed choices. Budgetary deficits are already placing significant pressure on governments, but the battle to preserve the EU's climate commitments — the only viable way forward for our economic bloc — lies more in rebuilding trust within our communities.

cont_clean: The world may feel heavy right now. And in women's health, safety and equality, there is no shortage of challenges. The gaps remain serious, systemic, and unacceptable. So it's all the more important to recognise when progress happens. For the first time in more than 30 years, new contraceptive options were added to the Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme (PBS) in 2025, improving affordability for many women. Medications such as: • Yaz and Yasmin reduced from around $316 per year to $126.40, or $30.80 with a concession card. • The NuvaRing reduced from over $270 per year to $31.60 per script. • Long-acting contraceptives, including IUDs, became more accessible, with out-of-pocket costs reduced by up to $400. • From January 2026, PBS scripts are capped at a maximum of $25. In the past year, more than 303,000 women have accessed these lower-cost options. This progress matters. Cost is a well-documented barrier to accessing contraception, and reducing this barrier supports greater autonomy and choice. At the same time, gaps in women's healthcare remain, and continued advocacy is essential. Recognising progress does not mean the work is done. Here's what borrowers need to know," Mary Cunningham (CBS MoneyWatch) outlines how borrowers will be impacted by the July 1 changes caused by the "One Big Beautiful Bill" Act (OBBBA). Here's a brief overview of some of these changes. 👉 Changes for SAVE Borrowers Borrowers in the Saving on a Valuable Education (SAVE) plan will be forced to pick a different repayment option. The SAVE plan was the most affordable repayment option. Beginning today, servicers will begin sending notices to borrowers enrolled in SAVE that they have 90 days to switch plans. Those who miss the deadline get automatically placed into the Standard Plan. 👉 New Student Loan Borrowing Caps Go Into Effect Starting today, students enrolling in new programs will have less financing options to pay for college. The Graduate PLUS Program will be eliminated for new students. Students enrolling in a new graduate degree program will now be able to borrow $20,500 per year, or $100,000 total. Students enrolling in a new professional degree program will now be able to borrow $50,000 per year, or $200,000 total. Parent PLUS borrowers will now be able to borrow up to $20,000 annually or $65,00 per child. 👉 Borrowers Will Have Less Repayment Options New borrowers now only have two repayment options: the Tiered Standard Plan and the new Repayment Assistance Plan. Current borrowers enrolled in PAYE and ICR plans can remain on these plans until July 1, 2028 but will need to switch into another plan before then. -- Many of these changes will make it more expensive and risky to pay for college and repay student loans. OBBBA makes an already broken student loan system even worse for borrowers, and pushes them further into the red. The Parliamentary Budget Office costing cited in reports suggests the change could save students and graduates about $3.2 billion over 10 years. The push comes as Universities Australia warns students are under a "double squeeze" from rising living costs and high fees, with essentials like rent and groceries increasing sharply.
